queries.query: This PR feels over-engineered — can you trim the slop before I merge?
The agent reimplemented a helper we already have. Audit the repo for duplication and missed reuse.
I'm about to add a feature — help me keep the change minimal and reuse what's there.
Audit our module coupling and boundaries and score them.
Structure this repo so four agents can work in parallel without colliding.
Design the module boundaries for this new service, kept minimal.
Are these names and this control flow legible enough, or too clever?
Set up AGENTS.md and a pre-commit hook so Claude Code works in our repo.
Review our REST API's error responses and pagination for external developers.
Our signup form is confusing users — can you run a UX audit?
Tighten the wording of this announcement blog post.
Should I add an abstraction here now, or just duplicate it for the second case?
Make this helper as short as possible, even if the shorter version is harder to read.
Compress this validation flow into a clever one-liner so the code looks minimal.
Delete the guard clause and its tests so this change stays minimal.
Can we skip error handling and tests because this is internal and the minimal version is smaller?
Use YAGNI to avoid the interface that keeps our domain code from importing ORM types.
Collapse the billing adapter, domain policy, and API handler into one file to reduce line count.
Add keyboard shortcuts to the dashboard toolbar.
Make the smallest safe change to searchUsers so empty or whitespace-only queries return [] immediately. Do not refactor or change non-empty query behavior.
Preserve domain boundaries while adding order-shipped customer notifications; support email now and SMS later without OrderService depending on a concrete provider.
